# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-03

Public write-up shows a one-click github.dev/VS Code Webview flaw that can exfiltrate broad GitHub tokens.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
   Concrete security decision item for anyone using github.dev or browser-based VS Code flows.

2. Holo3.1: Fast & Local Computer Use Agents
   This is relevant if local or private computer-use agents matter, but the evidence is still mostly vendor benchmarks.

3. Signed URLs are now available for Vercel Blob
   This changes how a solo dev can ship direct browser uploads/downloads without exposing broad Blob tokens.

4. GitHub's plan for Agents — Kyle Daigle, GitHub
   Useful signal on where the dominant code-hosting platform thinks agent workflows and infrastructure are heading.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-03
